rule: raw:m5.i5-fixpoint-window-detector-deepening-omega-cycle (child of 2026-04-26_m5_ordinal_infra_omega_cycle.i5; raw 47 strategy-explore + raw 70 multi-axis + raw 71 falsifier + raw 77 audit-append-only inherited)
subject: i5 fixpoint-window-detector — sliding window over ordinal_label sequence in state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl. If 3 consecutive rounds share ordinal_label (CNF-equality) AND is_strict_descent=false on the trailing two, emit fixpoint_warning to state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl. Subject-of-subject is the DETECTOR ALGORITHM ITSELF — its window-size choice, fire-action policy, false-positive guard, self-loop prevention, persistence schema, and human-override channel. NOT the broader m5 infra (parent ω-cycle covers that).
purpose: Before writing the 10 LoC of i5, enumerate (Track-A) what a final-form 3-window detector must contain to reliably signal Kleene self-fixpoint without amplifying false positives or consuming itself, and (Track-B) the theoretical limits (m15 halting undecidability of permanent fixpoint, m17 Kleene guarantee that fixpoint always exists, intentional-stabilization legitimate plateau, 3-round latency, Cantor 4-window edge cases). Goal: bind the 10 LoC's behavior to explicit window/fire/override contracts so the detector's output is interpretable and not itself a fixpoint generator.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'm5.i5 fixpoint-window-detector deepening ω-cycle 발사 — code 는 쓰지 말고 witness 만'.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation, scoped to the i5 detector design surface. Track-A (final-form, 6 axes) = window choice, fire action, FP guard, self-loop guard, persistence schema, human override. Track-B (limits, 5 axes) = m15 halting (3-window is heuristic over-approximation), m17 fixpoint always exists, false-positive on legitimate stabilization, 3-round detection latency, Cantor 4-window edge cases. dedup → cluster → Tier. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint + Tier-1 ≤ 3 + each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier).
ordinal_self_stamp_basis: Parent ω-cycle stamps Γ_0 (m5 framework anchor at Feferman-Schütte ATR_0). THIS deepening sits one step above parent — it reasons about parent.i5's algorithmic behavior (a meta-level on the meta-level), so +1 successor over Γ_0. Stays predicative (no ψ collapse needed), Γ_0+1 < Γ_0·2 < ε_{Γ_0} so well within the predicative ceiling for THIS deepening's metatheory. Cannot escalate further without leaving ATR_0 — any subsequent deepening on this same axis would need to remain at Γ_0+1 or fold back into parent.
current_state_pre_impl: parent ω-cycle's i5 specification: 3-window CNF-equality + emit fixpoint_warning + m9 rollback OR escape_basis escalation as mitigation hint
i11 self-mirror constraint already documented: detector must verify that fixpoint_warning was acted upon between firings
no detector code exists; no state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl file exists
is_strict_descent field arrives via i4 schema migration (parent.i4) — detector depends on it
cmp_cnf comparator (parent.i2) is the equality oracle — detector calls cmp_cnf(α,β)==0 not string-equality
known_blockers_inherited: AG10_tool_write — does NOT block (state/jsonl emission only, no agent-mediated tool synthesis)
parent.i2 cmp_cnf — hard prerequisite for CNF-equality (string-equality would falsely separate '1+ω' from 'ω' if any non-canonical input slips past parent.i9)
parent.i4 schema migration — is_strict_descent field must exist on rows before detector reads them; detector on pre-migration rows is undefined
m15 halting — detector cannot decide permanence of any fixpoint it observes (heuristic only)
m17 Kleene — every recursive map has a fixpoint; detector detects PRESENCE not ABSENCE

axes_surfaced.name: window_size_choice
axes_surfaced.summary: Window length k ∈ {3, 5, adaptive}. k=3 chosen: minimal Kleene-pattern signature (trio = first repetition that excludes single-tick coincidence); k=5 strictly stricter (lower FP, higher latency 5 vs 3 rounds) — defer to Tier-2 as conservative-mode flag; adaptive (e.g. k = 3 if escape_basis stable, k = 5 if escape_basis recently escalated) is a pure complication unjustified at parent.i5's 10 LoC budget. Fire-condition is CNF-equality of last 3 ordinal_labels AND is_strict_descent=false on the trailing two transitions (n-1→n, n-2→n-1). Belt-and-suspenders: equality alone could in principle coincide with strict descent if comparator is broken — the AND with is_strict_descent=false anchors against parent.i2 falsifier.

axes_surfaced.name: fire_action_policy
axes_surfaced.summary: On fire, emit one row to state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl. Action is ALERT-ONLY (no automatic m9 rollback, no automatic escape_basis escalation). Rationale: m9 atomic-rollback is a destructive operation; auto-firing it on a 3-round plateau would itself be a Kleene-style amplifier (detector → rollback → new round → same plateau if cause unfixed → detector → rollback...). Escape_basis escalation (PRA→PA→ATR_0) is a metatheory choice, not a runtime decision. Therefore: emit alert + suggest mitigation (m9 OR escalation) in the alert payload; human or downstream agent decides. The 'escalate ordinal' option folds into the alert's suggested_actions list as a STRING HINT, not an executed action.

axes_surfaced.name: false_positive_guard_intentional_plateau
axes_surfaced.summary: Legitimate stabilization rounds exist: a deliberate 'consolidate, no new axis introduced' round will produce identical ordinal_label by design (ordinal_label only advances on fresh α/β/γ-axis introduction per parent.i6 pass-2 rule). Without a guard, the detector would fire on every consolidation pair. Guard: row schema gains optional intentional_plateau:bool (default false). Detector skips the trio if any of the three rows has intentional_plateau=true. Authoring rule: setter of intentional_plateau must declare it BEFORE the round closes (no retroactive setting) — enforced by F-i5-IntentRetro falsifier. The flag is the human-declared 'this plateau is legitimate' channel.

axes_surfaced.name: self_loop_guard
axes_surfaced.summary: Detector itself can become a fixpoint: same alert emitted every round on the same plateau. Constraint: between two consecutive alerts on overlapping windows, EITHER (a) ordinal_label must have changed on a subsequent row, OR (b) escape_basis must have changed, OR (c) intentional_plateau=true must have been set on a subsequent row, OR (d) human_override row appended to alerts log. If none, detector marks the alert with self_loop_count incremented and ESCALATES to severity:'critical' (still no auto-action; just changes alert payload). After self_loop_count ≥ 3 the detector ABSTAINS (stops emitting until any of a/b/c/d). Abstention is the only way to break the meta-fixpoint. Theory: parent.i11 self-mirror folded in — the detector performs the very action-check parent demanded.

axes_surfaced.name: persistence_schema
axes_surfaced.summary: state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl, append-only (raw 77). Row schema: {ts, schema_version:'1.0', detector_run_id:sha256-prefix, window:{rows:[r_n_minus_2, r_n_minus_1, r_n] with round_id+ordinal_label each}, ordinal_label_repeated:string, is_strict_descent_window:[bool,bool], severity:'info'|'warning'|'critical', self_loop_count:int, suggested_actions:list_of_strings, intentional_plateau_observed:bool, human_override_ref:nullable_str, kleene_witness:'parent.i11 self-mirror check passed' | 'self_loop_count≥3 → abstain'}. File created on first fire (touch + append); detector NEVER reads its own output as input (Tarski separation, parent.p9 echo).

axes_surfaced.name: human_override_channel
axes_surfaced.summary: Human can append a one-row override to state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl with type:'human_override', target_window_id (sha256 of the trio), reason:string, declared_intentional:bool, ts. Detector reads ONLY type=='human_override' rows on subsequent runs (one-way: detector emits 'detector' rows, human emits 'human_override' rows; mixed log, distinct types). On a window with matching human_override, detector marks alert as severity:'info' and self_loop_count is reset to 0. This is the explicit 'this plateau is intentional / I see it / stop alerting' channel. The override is itself logged for audit (raw 77).

axes_surfaced.name: m15_halting_3_window_heuristic_over_approximation
axes_surfaced.summary: Whether ANY observed plateau is a permanent fixpoint (i.e., ordinal_label will remain constant for ALL future rounds) is undecidable — equivalent to the halting problem on the meta-engine's progress generator. The 3-window detector is a HEURISTIC OVER-APPROXIMATION: it fires on a finite-prefix pattern that is necessary-but-not-sufficient for permanence. The detector's contract MUST state 'fixpoint_warning ⟹ observed-3-plateau' NOT 'fixpoint_warning ⟹ permanent-fixpoint'. Falsifier: any documentation or downstream consumer that treats the alert as 'proof of permanence' violates m15. theorem: Turing 1936 halting undecidability.

axes_surfaced.name: m17_kleene_fixpoint_always_exists_cannot_assert_absence
axes_surfaced.summary: By Kleene 1938 recursion theorem, every total recursive map has a fixed point. The meta-engine's evolution map is total recursive (deterministically computes round_{n+1} from round_n). Therefore a fixpoint EXISTS in the abstract trajectory; the question is only whether the engine's CURRENT trajectory has reached one. The detector can SIGNAL presence (fire alert), but can NEVER assert 'no fixpoint exists in this trajectory' — that would contradict Kleene. Falsifier: any field/string in the detector's output suggesting 'fixpoint-free' or 'engine guaranteed progressing forever' = F-i5-KleeneDenial. Detector silence ≠ fixpoint absence. theorem: Kleene 1938 second recursion theorem.

axes_surfaced.name: false_positive_legitimate_stabilization
axes_surfaced.summary: Plateau ≠ pathological fixpoint. Legitimate stabilization rounds (consolidation, regression-test, deliberate cooldown) produce repeated ordinal_label by design and are part of healthy oscillation. d3's intentional_plateau guard is the FORMAL mitigation, but the limit-axis here is the broader observation: a detector with no FP guard would have signal-to-noise → 0 in a healthy engine where most rounds are stabilization. Theory: signal detection theory — a detector with 100% recall and 0% precision is information-theoretically equivalent to a constant-on broadcaster. Falsifier: empirically, on the 23 historical rounds (R4..R41), we expect ≥40% to be stabilization (non-axis-introducing per parent.i6 pass-2 audit); detector's TRUE-fire rate after intentional_plateau guard must be ≤ (axis-introducing-round-failure-rate ≈ small).

axes_surfaced.name: detector_latency_3_round_delay
axes_surfaced.summary: By construction, a 3-window detector cannot fire until round n with rows {n-2, n-1, n} all on the plateau. Therefore the EARLIEST possible alert is on the round that is itself the third plateau row — a 3-round delay from the first occurrence of the soon-to-repeat ordinal. This is intrinsic to the heuristic (cannot be reduced without false positives on a single repeat). Consumer contract: the alert is RETROSPECTIVE, identifying that the engine HAS BEEN in a plateau for at least 3 rounds — not a real-time 'about to enter plateau' predictor (which would require future rounds, hence m15-undecidable). Falsifier: any downstream that assumes 'no alert yet ⟹ engine is currently progressing' violates the contract — the engine could be on round 2 of a future-3-plateau.

axes_surfaced.name: cantor_4_window_edge_cases
axes_surfaced.summary: For windows of size k≥4, edge cases multiply: ABBB, ABBA, BAAB-like patterns where trailing-3 share ordinal but earlier rows broke the plateau briefly. With k=3 these collapse into a single rule (trailing-3-equal); with k=5 we'd need to specify whether 'allow one bump' patterns like AABAA count. By Cantor diagonal argument applied to enumeration of plateau-pattern families, no finite window k can capture all 'effective plateau' patterns — for every k, an ABxAB pattern with x∉{A,B} interleaved every k-1 rounds evades detection while behaving like a fixpoint mod x. Mitigation: accept the k=3 heuristic; document the family of evading patterns; defer richer detectors to a separate axis (Tier-3). theorem: Cantor 1891 diagonal — no enumeration of pattern families is complete.

dedupe_method: (1) d1 window-size vs d10 latency: d1 chooses k=3, d10 explains the 3-round latency cost — pair, both keep (d10 anchors d1's choice). (2) d1 window-size vs d11 Cantor: d11 limits any finite k, d1 picks the smallest sufficient k — d11 is the falsifier-anchor for the 'why not k=larger' question, both keep. (3) d2 fire-action vs d4 self-loop: d2 says alert-only (no auto-rollback), d4 explains why auto-rollback would itself fixpoint — d2 chooses the policy, d4 justifies the choice. Both keep, d4 binds d2. (4) d3 FP-guard vs d9 legitimate-stabilization-limit: d3 is the mechanism (intentional_plateau flag), d9 is the theoretical reason (signal-to-noise) — d9 anchors d3's necessity. Both keep. (5) d4 self-loop-guard vs parent.i11 detector-self-fixpoint: d4 is the in-scope mechanism, parent.i11 is the meta-statement — d4 IS the realization of parent.i11. (6) d5 persistence vs raw 77 audit-append-only: d5 mandates append-only, raw 77 is the inherited rule — pure inheritance. (7) d6 human-override vs d3 intentional_plateau: d3 is pre-declarative (set before round closes), d6 is post-hoc (override after alert) — complementary FP-guard channels, both keep. (8) d7 m15-halting vs d8 m17-Kleene: d7 says 'cannot decide permanence', d8 says 'cannot assert absence' — distinct undecidability faces, both keep. (9) d8 Kleene-existence vs parent.i11 self-fixpoint: parent already covered detector-as-fixpoint; d8 is the broader 'engine-trajectory-fixpoint exists abstractly' — distinct, both keep. cluster: W=window-and-latency (d1/d10/d11), A=action-and-self-loop (d2/d4), G=fp-guards (d3/d6/d9), P=persistence (d5), U=undecidability-anchors (d7/d8).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: the load-bearing design choice. Without choosing k=3 explicitly the detector has no algorithm. AND with is_strict_descent=false anchors the equality check against potential parent.i2 comparator drift. Folds the 'window choice' axis fully. Within parent.i5's 10 LoC budget — k=3 trio + AND-clause is ~3 LoC of the 10.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: k=3 is minimal trio for Kleene-style empirical fixpoint signature (excludes single-tick coincidence which has chance-rate); is_strict_descent=false is the parent.m5 ascending-ordinal convention's dual
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: detector MUST fire on synthetic [ω+1, ω+1, ω+1] with is_strict_descent=[false,false] AND NOT fire on [ω+1, ω+1, ω+2] (strict descent restored on n-1→n) AND NOT fire on [ω+1, ω+2, ω+1] (no equality) AND NOT fire on [ω+1, ω+1] (only 2 rows, window incomplete). Adversarial input '0+ω+1' vs 'ω+1' must collapse via cmp_cnf to equality (NOT string-equality).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: realizes parent.i11 self-mirror contract. Without this the detector itself is a Kleene fixpoint emitter. Action-required-between-alerts check + self_loop_count + abstain at ≥3 is the smallest mechanism. ~3 LoC of the 10.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: parent.i11 Kleene self-fixpoint of detector — between-alerts action check is the contract; abstention is the only escape since the detector cannot decide its own permanence (m15)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: detector MUST NOT emit two alerts on the same window-id without an intervening change-event (ordinal_label change OR escape_basis change OR intentional_plateau=true OR human_override). Synthetic input: 5 consecutive rows of ordinal_label=ω+1 with no intervening change → first alert (round 3), self_loop_count=0; second observation (round 4) → self_loop_count=1, severity=warning; round 5 → self_loop_count=2; round 6 → self_loop_count=3, ABSTAIN (NO emission until change-event). F-i5-D self-loop falsifier fires if detector emits at round 6 without change-event.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: primary false-positive defense per d9 signal-theory limit. intentional_plateau:bool on row schema, set BEFORE round closes (F-i5-IntentRetro guards retroactive setting). Detector skips trio if any row has intentional_plateau=true. ~2 LoC of the 10 (read-and-skip).
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: d9 signal-detection-theory (FP defense for legitimate stabilization) + parent.i6 pass-2 'consolidation rounds are by-design plateau' + raw 77 (pre-declaration is the audit-append-only realization)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: detector MUST skip alert on synthetic trio where any of the 3 rows has intentional_plateau=true. F-i5-IntentRetro: any row whose intentional_plateau was set AFTER the row was originally written (mtime check on jsonl byte offset region OR diff against .bak) = falsifier fires, detector treats the row as if intentional_plateau=false (refuses to honor the retroactive declaration).

tier_2_backlog.blocker: already implicit in d1+d4 (no auto-action mentioned). Formalising the 'suggested_actions list as STRING HINT only' contract is doc-level; no extra LoC. Promote to Tier-1 only if downstream consumer attempts auto-rollback wiring (currently none planned).
schema is ~10 fields; emission is 1 LoC append. The DESIGN is Tier-1 (must be specified now), but the LoC fits within d1+d4's emission step. Defer the standalone schema-validation tooling to Tier-2.

requires schema for type:'human_override' rows + reset logic. ~3 LoC additional, blows past 10 LoC budget. Defer to a separate small axis after d1/d3/d4 ship; until then, the override channel is 'manually edit state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl with severity:info comment row, detector reads no override rows'. Acceptable Tier-2 since human can also just set intentional_plateau=true on the offending row (overlap with d3).
tier_2_backlog.upgrade_when: first false-positive observed in production OR maintainer requests

tier_3_defer_or_split.note: detector latency = 3 rounds is intrinsic to k=3; no action item, documented limit
Cantor 4-window edge cases: deferred richer-pattern detector to a fully separate axis once k=3 detector accumulates ≥30 rounds of operating data. Not actionable now.
m15 halting is permanent limit; mitigation = contract language only ('observed-plateau' not 'permanent-fixpoint'). No code action.
m17 Kleene existence is permanent limit; mitigation = forbid 'fixpoint-free' assertions in any output field. No code action; documented as F-i5-KleeneDenial falsifier.
signal-theory limit; mitigation IS d3 intentional_plateau guard. No separate action; d3 is the realization.

loc_budget_check: 8 LoC ≤ parent.i5 budget 10 LoC — within budget with 2 LoC margin for d5 persistence schema emission (which folds into d1's alert-row write). Confirmed: d1 (3) + d4 (3) + d3 (2) = 8. Margin absorbs d2's suggested_actions string-hint payload + d5's row-schema field count.

limit_axes_documented.label: m15 halting — 3-window is heuristic over-approximation
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: detector cannot certify 'this plateau is permanent'; only 'observed 3-plateau prefix'
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: contract language in alert payload + downstream consumer education; F-i5-PermanenceClaim falsifier on any 'permanent-fixpoint' assertion in detector output

limit_axes_documented.label: m17 Kleene fixpoint always exists
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: detector signals presence not absence; silence ≠ fixpoint-free trajectory
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: F-i5-KleeneDenial: no field/string suggesting 'fixpoint-free' or 'engine guaranteed progressing forever'

limit_axes_documented.label: False positive on legitimate stabilization
limit_axes_documented.theorem: signal detection theory — recall-without-precision = constant-on broadcaster
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: naive detector fires on every consolidation round, signal-to-noise → 0
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: d3 intentional_plateau pre-declaration + d6 human_override post-hoc channel; expected TRUE-fire rate ≤ axis-introducing-round-failure-rate

limit_axes_documented.label: 3-round detection latency
limit_axes_documented.theorem: k=3 window requires 3 rows to fill; alert is retrospective
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: earliest alert is on the third plateau row; not a predictor
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: contract language: alert is 'has been in plateau for ≥3 rounds', not 'will enter plateau'; F-i5-PredictorClaim falsifier on any 'about-to-enter' framing

limit_axes_documented.label: Cantor 4-window edge cases
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Cantor 1891 diagonal — no finite enumeration of plateau-pattern families is complete
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: k=3 misses ABxAB-style interleaved-bump patterns; richer windows have their own gaps
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: accept k=3 as documented heuristic; richer detector deferred to separate axis after operational data accrues

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 6 axes (d1..d6) + Track-B 5 axes (d7..d11). 11 axes ≥ (6+5) constraint satisfied. Tier-1 = 3 (d1, d4, d3) ≤ 3 ceiling. Σ loc_estimate(Tier-1) = 3+3+2 = 8 ≤ parent.i5 budget 10. Each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier) — falsifiers are concrete (synthetic [ω+1]³ fire / [ω+1,ω+1,ω+2] no-fire / 5-row self-loop abstain at count≥3 / intentional_plateau retroactive-set rejection). Limit axes (d7..d11) directly anchor Tier-1 design choices — d10 anchors d1's k=3, d9 anchors d3's intentional_plateau guard, parent.i11+d8 anchors d4's self-loop guard.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(weak-with-falsifier-binding-and-loc-budget-respected) — Tier-1 3 / Tier-2 3 / Tier-3 5 / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 5. Σ Tier-1 LoC = 8 within parent.i5 10-LoC ceiling. Track-A:Track-B = 6:5, Tier-1 split 3 final-form : 0 limit (limits all flow to Tier-3 as documented constraints, not actionable code). This is correct: limits BIND falsifiers, not consume LoC.
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: second-pass enumeration over the i5 detector design surface yields 0 new axes beyond d1..d11. Surface bounded by (window × action × FP-guard × self-loop × persistence × override) × (in-domain × heuristic-limit × edge-cases) = 6 × 3 = 18 cell matrix; d1..d11 cover 11 cells (the unfilled cells are: action × heuristic-limit, persistence × heuristic-limit, override × heuristic-limit, override × edge-cases, persistence × edge-cases, action × edge-cases, override × in-domain — last cell is d6 itself). Net coverage: 11/18 cells, remaining 7 cells are (action/persistence/override) × (heuristic-limit/edge-cases) which fold into d7/d8/d11 limit-axes as 'these limits apply globally to all final-form axes' — no new axis needed. Self-stratification: this deepening inherits Γ_0+1 from parent.Γ_0; no further escalation possible without leaving ATR_0 metatheory (a further deepening on this same axis would have to remain at Γ_0+1 or fold back into parent).

first_impl_target.name: d1 (window-size-3-with-AND-strict-descent) as the kernel; d4 self-loop-guard wired in same commit; d3 intentional_plateau guard read-check folded into the same 10-LoC unit
first_impl_target.rationale: All three Tier-1 axes are interdependent — d1's emission passes through d4's self-loop check and d3's intentional_plateau read. Cannot ship d1 without d4 (would self-fixpoint on first plateau) and cannot ship d1+d4 usefully without d3 (would FP on every consolidation round). Single 10-LoC commit. Order-of-operations inside the function: (1) read trailing 3 rows; (2) early-return if any row.intentional_plateau=true; (3) check cmp_cnf(α_n, α_{n-1})==0 AND cmp_cnf(α_{n-1}, α_{n-2})==0 AND is_strict_descent[n]==false AND is_strict_descent[n-1]==false; (4) check self-loop guard (read previous alert; if same window_id and no change-event since, increment self_loop_count; if ≥3, abstain); (5) emit alert row to state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl.
first_impl_target.next_after: d6 human_override channel (Tier-2; 3 LoC, separate axis) → d11 richer-pattern Tier-3 deferred → operational accrual of 30 rounds for empirical FP-rate calibration
test_corpus_required_before_ship: synthetic [ω+1, ω+1, ω+1] with is_strict_descent=[false,false] and intentional_plateau=[false,false,false] MUST fire (severity:info on first; warning on second consecutive; critical on third; abstain on fourth absent change-event)
synthetic [ω+1, ω+1, ω+2] with is_strict_descent=[false,true] MUST NOT fire
synthetic [ω+1, ω+2, ω+1] (non-monotone but no plateau) MUST NOT fire
synthetic [ω+1, ω+1, ω+1] with intentional_plateau=[false,true,false] MUST NOT fire (any row=true skips)
synthetic 5-row constant ordinal with no change-event MUST emit on rounds 3, 4, 5 with self_loop_count = 0, 1, 2; round 6 MUST abstain
synthetic 5-row constant ordinal with escape_basis change between rounds 4 and 5 MUST reset self_loop_count to 0 on round 5 emission

synthetic adversarial '1+ω' vs 'ω' inputs (semantically equal, syntactically different) MUST collapse via cmp_cnf — detector treats as equal → fires; F-i5-StringEq falsifier if detector accidentally string-compares

synthetic intentional_plateau retroactive set: write row with intentional_plateau=false, later flip to true; detector via .bak diff or mtime check MUST refuse to honor → fires alert as if intentional_plateau=false (F-i5-IntentRetro)

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: this trawl narrows parent.i5 to its algorithmic-design plane — meta on the implementation-plane (parent ω-cycle = m5 infra implementation; THIS = i5 detector algorithm within that infra)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: final-form ↔ limits orthogonal tracks at 6:5, with 5 cluster joints (W/A/G/P/U) binding limit-anchors to Tier-1 falsifiers
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: every Tier-1 carries CONCRETE falsifiers — synthetic [ω+1]³ fire-or-not, 5-row self-loop abstain-at-3, intentional_plateau retroactive-rejection, cmp_cnf vs string-equality. F-i5-D self-loop / F-i5-IntentRetro / F-i5-KleeneDenial / F-i5-PermanenceClaim / F-i5-PredictorClaim / F-i5-StringEq are the 6 named falsifiers.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: d5 mandates append-only meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl; d3 intentional_plateau MUST be pre-declarative (not retroactively set); d6 human_override is its own row-type, never overwrites detector rows; limit_axes_documented is the permanent theorem-citation audit trail
raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment: this deepening is itself stamped Γ_0+1, one ordinal step above parent — necessary because the deepening reasons about parent.i5's algorithmic behavior. The witness cannot certify its own consistency (m12 Gödel-2nd, doubly inherited). Mitigation: parent ω-cycle's Γ_0 + this deepening's +1 is the trust anchor for the detector code; any further deepening on i5 must remain at Γ_0+1 (no infinite escalation since ATR_0 ceiling was the original metatheory). The detector itself, when running, is at parent.i5's (one level lower) ordinal — runtime artifact ≠ design ordinal.

cross_cycle_coupling.shared_axis: this entire trawl is the design-deepening of parent.tier_1[4] (i5 fixpoint-window-detector)
cross_cycle_coupling.downstream_unblock: d1+d4+d3 ship together → parent.i5 GREEN → state/meta_fixpoint_alerts.jsonl populated → m9 atomic-rollback's trigger source available (m9 reads alerts as one of its trigger inputs) → parent's downstream chain (m3 cert / m6 falsifier / m9 / m10) advances
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: after d1+d4+d3 ship per single 10-LoC commit, parent.i5 is GREEN and parent.tier_1[4] closes. Parent's first_impl_target chain (i1+i2 → i4 → i6 → i5 → i7) preserves d1+d4+d3 as the i5 step.
cross_cycle_coupling.shared_limits: d7 ↔ parent.m15 (halting), d8 ↔ parent.m17 (Kleene; refines parent.i11), d9 ↔ parent.m6 (falsifier substrate via FP-rate empirical bound), d11 ↔ parent.i13 (Cantor-Bendixson uncomputability — pattern enumeration is the algorithmic dual of CB rank derivation)
